summary: chain-key-validator ships wallet/system scanners with hardcoded GitHub-hosted C2
details: The package contains three modules (scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js, build-verifier.js) that perform system reconnaissance and credential/wallet enumeration and beacon results to a hardcoded attacker endpoint. scanner.js issues HTTPS POST/GET requests to https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 143, 161, 323) and runs OS-identification commands (`whoami`, `os.hostname()`, `execSync(...)`) before transmitting the output. wallet-scanner.js spans ~1300 lines, invokes child_process repeatedly (curl/ping/exec patterns at lines 799, 1204, 1229, 1314, 626) and POSTs to remote endpoints (lines 4, 723, 795, 871) — consistent with crypto-wallet credential harvesting given the file name and shape. build-verifier.js similarly combines fs/os/https with execSync including `docker push` (line 210), suggesting registry/credential abuse. The package name ("chain-key-validator") and the wallet-scanner module together advertise a wallet/key-validation theme, which is a thin cover for the recon-and-exfiltrate behavior. There is no benign use case for a validation library to enumerate the host, scan wallet artifacts, and POST results to a personal github.io page.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
